TCP and UDP are both transport layer protocols in the Internet Protocol Suite (TCP/IP), but they differ significantly in how they handle data transmission, offering different trade-offs between reliability and speed.
Feature
TCP (Transmission Control Protocol)
UDP (User Datagram Protocol)
Connection
Connection-oriented (requires a handshake)
Connectionless (sends data immediately)
Reliability
High (guarantees delivery via acknowledgments)
Low (no guarantee of delivery)

Comparison Overview

HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) and HTTPS (Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure) are both protocols used to transfer data over the World Wide Web, but they differ significantly in terms of security.
Here is a comparison of the two:
Security: HTTP transmits data in plain text, meaning anyone intercepting the communication can read the information (like passwords or credit card numbers). HTTPS uses an SSL/TLS certificate to encrypt the data, making it unreadable to unauthorized parties.
Authentication: HTTP provides no way to verify the identity of the server you are connecting to. HTTPS allows the server to prove its identity using a digital certificate, ensuring you are talking to the legitimate website.

REST vs GraphQL
REST gives you fixed endpoints. GraphQL lets the app ask for exactly the fields it needs.
Use REST when...
Use GraphQL when...
The API is simple and predictable
Screens need many related pieces of data
Caching should be straightforward
You want to avoid over-fetching data
Teams prefer standard HTTP patterns
Client teams need more query flexibility
Short version: REST is simpler; GraphQL is more flexible.

Arrays and linked lists are fundamental linear data structures, but they differ significantly in how they store and access data in memory.
#
Arrays
Storage: Elements are stored in contiguous (adjacent) memory locations.
Access: Provide O(1) (constant time) access because you can calculate the exact memory address of any element using its index

Yoga in Indian Tradition
Yoga is a discipline for training the body, breath, mind, and awareness.
Its roots are in ancient Indian spiritual and philosophical traditions. Classical yoga emphasizes calming the mind, ethical living, focus, meditation, and self-knowledge.
Modern yoga often highlights posture and fitness, but traditionally it is broader: it is a path toward balance, clarity, and liberation.
